text: [VISUAL CONTENT - Page 11]¥n1.  **Mathematical Equations:** None on this page¥n¥n2.  **Graphs and Charts:** None on this page¥n¥n3.  **Tables:** None on this page¥n¥n4.  **Diagrams and Schematics:**¥n    *   **Figure 1.1: Schematic drawing of a burner.** This figure illustrates a simplified burner design for combustion. The schematic shows fuel and primary air entering a section where they mix to form a premixed mixture. This mixture exits the burner, creating a flame. Secondary air is introduced around the flame, presumably to complete the combustion process. The flow directions of fuel and air are indicated. The diagram highlights the creation of a premixed region upstream of the flame.

title: Master_thesis_for_reacting_jet
text: [VISUAL CONTENT - Page 39]¥n1.  **Mathematical Equations:**¥n¥nThere are no explicit mathematical equations on this page.¥n¥n2.  **Graphs and Charts:**¥n¥nFigure 3.2: Computational domain and grid.¥n¥n*   **Axes:**¥n    *   x-axis: x/D (non-dimensional axial distance, scaled by nozzle diameter D)¥n    *   y-axis: r/D (non-dimensional radial distance, scaled by nozzle diameter D)¥n¥n*   **Trends and Key Data Points:**¥n    *   The figure illustrates a 2D axisymmetric computational domain used for simulating flow in a gas turbine combustor.¥n    *   A grid is presented which is finer near domain origin in the lower left.¥n    *   The domain extends from x/D = 0 to approximately x/D = 7.¥n    *   The radius of the computational domain is marked as Radius R_outer = 0.25L, where L is the length of the domain.¥n    *   A nozzle outlet is indicated at x/D = 0 and between r/D = 0.2 and r/D = 0.6.¥n    *   A symmetry axis is indicated at r/D = 0.¥n¥n*   **Conclusions:**¥n    *   The figure visualizes the spatial discretization scheme; grid resolution increases toward the nozzle outlet and centerline. Suggests that higher gradients are expected near these regions.¥n¥n3.  **Tables:**¥n¥nThere are no tables on this page.¥n¥n4.  **Diagrams and Schematics:**¥n¥nThe core of the figure is the structured grid superimposed upon a rectangular domain, representing the combustion chamber. The following are included as part of the domain schematic:¥n¥n*   **Nozzle Outlet:** Indicates the location of the fuel and air injection.¥n*   **Symmetry Axis:** Represents the centerline of the axisymmetric combustor.¥n*   **Domain Boundaries:** Define the spatial extent of the simulation.¥n*   **Coordinate System:** x/D and r/D.¥n*   **Length L:** Denotes to the axial distance to the outflow of the combustor domain and is used to define the outer radius.¥n*    **Grid Staggering:** The grid uses a staggered arrangement, implying that scalar quantities and velocity components are computed at different locations within each control volume. The scalar variables are computed at the gridpoints, whereas, the velocity variables are computed on the control-volume faces between the scalar-quantity gridpoints.

title: Master_thesis_for_reacting_jet
text: [VISUAL CONTENT - Page 40]¥n1.  **Equations:**¥n¥n    *   (rho_e * u_e * Phi_e - rho_w * u_w * Phi_w) * r * delta_r * delta_theta + (rho_n * v_n * Phi_n - rho_s * v_s * Phi_s) * delta_x * delta_theta - (Gamma_e * (del Phi / del x)_e - Gamma_w * (del Phi / del x)_w) * r * delta_theta * delta_r - (Gamma_n * (del Phi / del x)_n - Gamma_s * (del Phi / del x)_s) * delta_x * delta_theta = S * r * delta_x * delta_r * delta_theta¥n    *   Phi_n = (Phi_P + Phi_N) / 2¥n    *   (del Phi / del x)_e = (Phi_E - Phi_P) / delta_x_e¥n    *   (del Phi / del x)_w = (Phi_P - Phi_W) / delta_x_w¥n    *   (del Phi / del x)_n = (Phi_N - Phi_P) / delta_r_n¥n    *   (del Phi / del x)_s = (Phi_P - Phi_S) / delta_r_s¥n    *   A_P * Phi_P = A_E * Phi_E + A_W * Phi_W + A_N * Phi_N + A_S * Phi_S + S * r * delta_x * delta_r¥n    *   A_P = A_E + A_W + A_N + A_S¥n    *   (A_P - S'_P * r * delta_x * delta_r) * Phi_P = A_E * Phi_E + A_W * Phi_W + A_N * Phi_N + A_S * Phi_S + S_C * r * delta_x * delta_r¥n¥n2.  **Graphs and Charts:**¥n¥n    *   There is a diagram illustrating control volumes in a 2D structured grid (x-r plane) near the symmetry axis. Different shadings (yellow, horizontal lines, vertical lines) are used to indicate the control volume for the scalar variable Phi, velocity component u, and velocity component v respectively.  The gridlines represent the boundaries of the control volumes.  The control volume for the scalar variable Phi is centered at point P.¥n¥n3.  **Tables:**¥n¥n    *   There are no tables on the page.¥n¥n4.  **Diagrams and Schematics:**¥n¥n    *   A schematic labeled ¥

shows a section of a 2D grid.  The x and r axes are indicated.  The grid is partitioned into control volumes. The control volume for a scalar variable Phi is shaded yellow.  Control volumes for velocity components u and v are also shown with different hash mark patterns. The diagram is likely for illustrating a finite volume discretization scheme in a cylindrical coordinate system, near the symmetry axis.
